## OBJECTIVES
- IMMEDIATE OBJECTIVE: Confirm the core system tools exist and report their versions.
- OVERALL OBJECTIVE (section S00): Verify every required tool, library, and dependency is installed and actually working before any production step runs, emitting actionable diagnostics for anything missing.

## INPUTS YOU MAY READ
- this step file
- authority/00_MASTER_CONTRACT.md

## THE ONE ACTION (inspect_only step: no source files are modified; evidence records inspection)
Run the version probe for python3, git, curl, systemctl, node, npm; capture real output into the evidence artifact.

## COMMAND(S) TO RUN
```bash
for t in python3 git curl systemctl node npm; do printf '%s: ' "$t"; command -v "$t" && "$t" --version 2>&1 | head -1 || echo MISSING; done | tee ../evidence/tmp_tooling.txt
```

## EVIDENCE TO PRODUCE
- WRITE: evidence/S00-01_TOOLING_INVENTORY.json  (schema: contracts/evidence_schema.json)
- MUST CONTAIN: the exact commands run, their real stdout/stderr, the concrete
  files created/modified with paths and line ranges, and each acceptance-predicate
  result. No placeholders. No fabricated output.
- HASH: `python3 engine/hash_chain.py --append '#S00-01_TOOLING_INVENTORY' evidence/S00-01_TOOLING_INVENTORY.json <SUCCESS|FAILURE>`

## ACCEPTANCE PREDICATES (ALL must be true for SUCCESS)
- [ ] Every core tool resolves via `command -v` OR is explicitly recorded MISSING with a diagnostic.
- [ ] A version string is captured for each present tool.
- [ ] evidence/S00-01_TOOLING_INVENTORY.json lists each tool and its status.
